m.text.body: This room is intended for discussion, implementation, and MSC drafting of Presence v2 via Selective Presence. Selective Presence allows users to select which users they'd like their presence changes to be pushed to. This is done through UI elements allowing users to select a list of "friends" to share data with. Additionally, options such as room-wide defaults or DM based selection for who to trust by default. Their presence changes will be sent to only those users, reducing excessive network requests.

content.topic: This room is intended for discussion, implementation, and MSC drafting of Presence v2 via Selective Presence. Selective Presence allows users to select which users they'd like their presence changes to be pushed to. This is done through UI elements allowing users to select a list of "friends" to share data with. Additionally, options such as room-wide defaults or DM based selection for who to trust by default. Their presence changes will be sent to only those users, reducing excessive network requests.

content.body:
ok so looking at the algorithm itself

* "destination" is ambiguous as it is commonly used to refer to _destination servers_, not rooms
* Checking if the room is ignored by the user should probably be done before checking the room state, as checking the room state is a more expensive operation than just filtering out rooms that we know we won't care about
  
  * The allow list should also probably be skipped when checking hints, assuming this means to always assume presence is allowed
* why is there an exclusion set at all? Isn't exclusion implied by a lack of exclusion in the destination set?
* "Subdivide the destination and exclusion sets into homeservers" what?
* defining "low priority" here will probably slow the proposal down as you then have to elaborate on what low priority even means. that'd probably best as a precursor proposal if anything
* heartbeat bad, don't do that
* You shouldn't suggest implementation details
* "Resolve the accompanying destination and exclusion sets into the destination users on this server" that's a LOT of information and a huge process to unpack, stuffed into a single line. That should be broken down.
* Again, "low priority" will only cause problems here. This will also require rather large changes on the server-side to how sync even works, so probably won't end up being implemented
* "Clients are able to request the presence state of a user if it is available to them" I assume this re-uses the existing API endpoints for this?
content.formatted_body:
ok so looking at the algorithm itself

  • "destination" is ambiguous as it is commonly used to refer to destination servers, not rooms

  • Checking if the room is ignored by the user should probably be done before checking the room state, as checking the room state is a more expensive operation than just filtering out rooms that we know we won't care about

    • The allow list should also probably be skipped when checking hints, assuming this means to always assume presence is allowed

  • why is there an exclusion set at all? Isn't exclusion implied by a lack of exclusion in the destination set?

  • "Subdivide the destination and exclusion sets into homeservers" what?

  • defining "low priority" here will probably slow the proposal down as you then have to elaborate on what low priority even means. that'd probably best as a precursor proposal if anything

  • heartbeat bad, don't do that

  • You shouldn't suggest implementation details

  • "Resolve the accompanying destination and exclusion sets into the destination users on this server" that's a LOT of information and a huge process to unpack, stuffed into a single line. That should be broken down.

  • Again, "low priority" will only cause problems here. This will also require rather large changes on the server-side to how sync even works, so probably won't end up being implemented

  • "Clients are able to request the presence state of a user if it is available to them" I assume this re-uses the existing API endpoints for this?

m.text.body: This room is intended for discussion, implementation, and MSC drafting of Presence v2 via Selective Presence. Selective Presence allows users to select which users they'd like their presence changes to be pushed to. This is done through UI elements allowing users to select a list of "friends" to share data with. Additionally, options such as room-wide defaults or DM based selection for who to trust by default. Their presence changes will be sent to only those users, reducing excessive network requests.

Selective Presence allows users to select which users they'd like their presence changes to be pushed to. This is done through UI elements allowing users to select a list of "friends" to share data with. Additionally, options such as room-wide defaults or DM based selection for who to trust by default.
